content:
NIGHT
  • Night Reading: "Was Atman not within him? Was not the source within his own heart? One must find the source within one’s own Self, one must possess it. Everything else was seeking—a detour, an error."
  • Reflection: Did I notice my own "inner voice" today? What was it saying? How did the world around me answer it?


Answer: Looking at my morning intention, I would say I did take that action. I went out despite obstacles, and accomplished something. I got a job. I paid attention to moments of distress and tried to figure out when and what specifically was causing that distress. I have begun to be more conscious of the factors that cause distress between myself and Kevin. I have begun to become aware that what’s causing discomfort within my heart is having that distress between myself and Kevin. Kevin and I begun to argue today, however, I realized that I could either make it worse, or I could, for the sake of my own well being, just swallow my pride, and let him be. Many of his actions make me think. The inner voice within me yearns to speak out loud, however, I know based on that one momentary altercation between Kevin and I that as of this day, the people present in my world would not agree with this inner voice. I believe at the beginning of the day, there was most definitely a pull towards the comfortable past, however, after that altercation with Kevin, the remainder of the day, I was yearning for a bit of a better future and something bigger than an argument and feeling dow
context: From document: A 30-Day Journey with Siddhartha: Aligning the Path with Daily Life — chunk 6 (chunk 6/29)
